author: Christian Reynoso
title: Homemade granola
ingredients: ½ cup (65 grams) whole raw pecans
½ cup (65 grams) raw sunflower seeds
⅓ cup (65 grams) extra-virgin olive oil, plus more for serving
¼ cup peanut, pecan, cashew or walnut butter
½ cup (65 grams) whole raw pecans
½ cup (65 grams) raw sunflower seeds
⅓ cup (65 grams) extra-virgin olive oil, plus more for serving
¼ cup peanut, pecan, cashew or walnut butter

instructions:
Heat the oven to 300 degrees.
Add everything except the dates to a large bowl.
Using your hands or tongs, toss thoroughly — really get in there — so the peanut butter, sugar, cinnamon and salt are evenly distributed.
They’re the tiny guys, so they take a minute to get everywhere.
Spread the granola on a sheet pan and bake, stirring every 10 to 15 minutes, until golden and dry to the touch, about 45 minutes.
It won’t look very clumpy (maybe a little), and it won’t be crisp yet.
Let it cool completely.
It will firm up and become crunchy as it cools.
Once cool, store the granola in an airtight container.
Serve with milk or yogurt, a drizzle of olive oil and dates.

Serve with milk or yogurt, a drizzle of olive oil and dates.
description: A crunchy, not-too-sweet homemade granola built on nuts, seeds and oats — meant for everyday bowls and especially good with chopped dates.
